assertion.title: Planck templates solve eq50; scipy constant optimization and pow operator show no net benefit
assertion.statement: Adding Planck/Bose-Einstein templates with variable numerator (v1/(exp(v1/v2)-1)) reliably solves eq50 (Blackbody Peak/Wien). Scipy-based constant optimization (Nelder-Mead) applied as post-GP polish shows no measurable benefit — failing equations have structural issues, not constant issues. A safe pow(x,y) binary operator was tested but rejected: it pollutes the GP search space, causing 5 regressions while only solving 1 new equation (eq46/Lennard-Jones). Net result: 39/50 exact matches (same as baseline but with eq50 now consistently solved).

findings.planck_template: SUPPORTED — v1/(exp(v1/v2)-1) template consistently solves eq50 (Blackbody Peak/Wien) across all 5 benchmark runs
findings.scipy_constant_optimization: INCONCLUSIVE — Nelder-Mead optimization of constants in best tree never converted a near-miss to exact match in any run. Failing equations have wrong structure, not wrong constants.
findings.pow_operator: REFUTED — Adding pow(x,y) to BINARY_OPS causes 37/50 (down from 39/50). Even when restricted from random tree generation, still causes regressions. The search space expansion outweighs the benefit for Lennard-Jones.
findings.wave_superposition_templates: INCONCLUSIVE — 2A*cos(delta/2)*cos(omega*t+delta/2) template did not help solve eq41 in any run.
